figures.caption: Table 1: Overview of downstream tasks. (Acronyms: NAFLD - Non Alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease; LUAD - Lung Adenocarcinoma; LSCC - Lung Squamous Cell Carcinoma; ISUP - International Society of Urological Pathology.) See D.1 for more info.
Fig. 8: Attention maps from each SSL method for gallbladder ultrasounds, including both a benign and malignant example.
Fig. 9: Attention maps from each SSL method for ultrasounds of normal liver and non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NFLD).
Fig. 13: PCA Visualizations of two histopathology patches from three downstream examples.
Fig. 2: Example visualizations including attention maps, cosine similarity maps (anchor points denoted by red X), and PCA maps for both ultrasound and pathology.
Fig. 6: Example attention maps from DINOv3 and MAE comparing [CLS] token and mean patch attentions for an ultrasound example. I-JEPA mean patch attention map is provided as a reference.
Fig. 7: Example attention maps from DINOv3 and MAE comparing [CLS] token and mean patch attentions for a histopathology example. I-JEPA mean patch attention map is provided as a reference.

Fig. 12: Carotid artery ultrasound cosine similarity map for each model. The anchor point (red X) is placed on the boundary of the carotid artery to demonstrate I-JEPA’s ability to learn anatomical structures.
Fig. 11: Attention maps from each SSL method for ultrasounds of the carotid and the four chamber view of the heart.
Table 2: Performance comparison across SSL methods on eight ultrasound classification tasks averaged across five seeds. All values are reported as percentages (%).
Fig. 1: SSL pretext tasks on a lung image for (a) MAE, (b) I-JEPA, and (c) DINOv3.
Fig. 5: Attention heatmaps from each attention head of the final ViT layer across models pre-trained on histopathology data for three downstream datasets.
Fig. 10: Attention maps from each SSL method for thyroid ultrasounds. Both ultrasounds show benign nodules but of varying sizes. Nodules are denoted by the calipers.

Fig. 3: Attention heatmaps from each attention head of the final ViT layer across models pre-trained on ultrasounds. The dark oval area in the GBCU image is the gallbladder with the liver region to the left. The oval area of the Fatty Liver image is the kidney, the white line above is the diaphragm, and the shaded region to the right is the liver. The TN5000 image contains a thyroid nodule marked with calipers.
Table 4: Comparison of Hyperparameters across three Models. Learning rate peak for DINOv3 histopathology model was 1× 10−5 otherwise all parameters were shared across ultrasound and histopathology.
